items.q: Why can a cold winter NOT disprove climate change?
items.correct: Climate is the long-term statistical average; a single cold winter is a weather event. Climate change is the trend in averages and extremes over decades, not the absence of cold days.
items.misconception: A really cold winter proves global warming is not happening.
items.random_wrong: Climate change only happens in summer.
items.why: This is one of the most common public misconceptions. Climate is the slope of the trend over 30+ years; individual weather events are scatter around that line — both can be cold.

items.q: Why do people move from rural to urban areas in LICs?
items.correct: A combination of push factors (rural poverty, lack of services, agricultural change) and pull factors (perceived jobs, education, healthcare) — though reality often disappoints.
items.misconception: Because cities in poor countries are always better than the countryside.
items.random_wrong: Because the government forces them to move to cities.
items.why: Migrants often end up in shanty towns worse than what they left. The decision is based on perception of opportunity, not guaranteed improvement — a key nuance students miss.

items.q: What drives the movement of tectonic plates?
items.correct: Convection currents in the mantle combined with slab pull at subduction zones and ridge push at constructive boundaries.
items.misconception: The Earth's rotation makes the plates move.
items.random_wrong: Plates move because of ocean tides pulling on the crust.
items.why: Students conflate the Earth spinning with crustal motion. Plate motion is driven by heat-driven convection in the mantle, not rotation — rotation only affects things like the Coriolis effect.

items.q: What is the population threshold for a megacity?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: 10 million. “1 million” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: In which country is Lagos located?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Nigeria. “Kenya” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Roughly what is the population of Lagos (millions)?
items.why: Exact answer: 15. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
